economic_notes: Deer Lake First Nation operates Deer Cable (cable, phone, internet services), Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (gaming revenue sharing at 1.70% of provincial gross gaming revenue), and Economic Development segment. Revenue from OFNLP is restricted to community development, health, education, cultural and economic development expenditures.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020 (not 2024). Province is Ontario. The statement consolidates 13 departments/segments including Deer Cable and OFNLP. Housing expenses combined Band Housing ($1,279,545) and CMHC Housing ($353,882) = $1,633,427. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $16,709,361 + Ministry of Community and Social Services $2,507,102 + CMHC $457,451 + SLAAMB $227,561 + Ministry of Health $310,696 + OFNLP $1,334,491 (treated as government transfer per agreement with Province of Ontario) less deferred revenue adjustments not separately allocable — using gross line items: $16,709,361 + $2,507,102 + $1,334,491 + $457,451 + $227,561 + $310,696 = $21,546,662 before deferred; OSR includes Rent $572,450 + Other revenue $2,349,414 net of deferred items. Net financial assets is positive $236,865 (not net debt). Accumulated surplus includes investment in tangible capital assets $17,890,673, operating surplus $3,019,978, and restricted reserves. No remuneration schedule included in the provided text. Capital segment expenses were $0 (actual) vs $303,900 budget.
